mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 3.5% indicates tightening labor market conditions, which historically correlate with elevated equity risk premiums and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in triggered periods show negative or neutral performance (mean < 0.1% or t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly acceleration in GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% signals robust economic expansion, driving corporate earnings optimism and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in high-GDP growth periods fall below 1.5% or show negative performance (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) and falling Fed funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 4.5%) creates a 'stagflation' risk scenario, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in triggered periods show positive performance (mean > 0.5%) or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO < 100) signals weakening manufacturing activity, which historically correlates with negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO contraction periods show positive performance (mean > 0.3%) or t-test p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST < 1,500k) indicates weak consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors price in slower economic growth.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in HOUST decline periods show positive performance (mean > 0.2%) or Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly surge in payroll employment (PAYEMS > 200k) signals strong labor market dynamics, which historically correlate with positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-PAYEMS periods show negative performance (mean < -0.3%) or t-test p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in consumer confidence (RSAFS < 680k) and rising housing starts (HOUST > 1,550k) creates conflicting demand signals, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in triggered periods show positive performance (mean > 1.0%) or Rank IC < 0.02.
